claimText: The 15th Conference of Parties (CoP15) to the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands was held in Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe.
evidenceExcerpt: Ramsar CoP15 was held in Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe with the theme 'Protecting Wetlands for Our Common Future'.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent environmental summits.

claimText: Under the Ramsar Convention, the 'wise use' of wetlands is defined as maintaining their ecological character using ecosystem-based approaches within sustainable development.
evidenceExcerpt: 'Wise use' means maintaining ecological character using ecosystem-based approaches within sustainable development.
examUse: Conceptual statement-based question on environmental terminology.
distractorHooks: 'Wise use' means complete prohibition of human activity
'Wise use' refers strictly to limiting water extraction for agriculture

claimText: The Ramsar Convention was adopted in 1971 and entered into force in 1975, with India officially joining as a member in 1982.
evidenceExcerpt: Adopted in 1971 in Ramsar, Iran; entered into force in 1975; India joined in 1982.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's participation in international environmental treaties.
distractorHooks: India was a founding member of the Ramsar Convention in 1971

claimText: The Secretariat of the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands is located in Gland, Switzerland.
evidenceExcerpt: Secretariat is located in Gland, Switzerland.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching environmental conventions to their secretariat locations.
distractorHooks: Secretariat is located in Nairobi, Kenya
Secretariat is located in Bonn, Germany
